sources.title: Guidance on the Development, Evaluation, and Application of Environmental Models
creators: U.S. Environmental Protection Agency, Council for Regulatory Environmental Modeling
sources.locator: Executive summary; sections 3.1 and 4.2.3; glossary; Appendix D.5, especially report pages ix, 14-16, 26-31, 43-46, and 69-74
sources.license: EPA website copyright and disclaimer terms; used only as a factual reference

uses.rightsBasisEvidence: Only factual definitions and modeling relationships were consulted. The lesson's wording, structure, examples, questions, diagram, calculations, and assessment were independently created; no EPA expression, media, figure, table, or dataset is reused.

agentAccess.notes: The EPA disclaimer and current robots route were checked before substantive access. The public report path was not disallowed, no agent-specific prohibition was found, and no login, paywall, rate limit, or technical control was bypassed.

sources.title: Equation of State
sources.locator: Ideal Gas section: the relations PV/T = nR and PV = nRT, variable descriptions, and the stated ideal-gas applicability boundary
sources.license: NASA public information under the NASA website disclaimer; used only as a factual reference

uses.rightsBasisEvidence: The source was used to verify the ideal-gas relation and its named quantities. No NASA prose, image, graph, exercise, example, or other expression is copied, adapted, or redistributed.

agentAccess.notes: NASA's disclaimer and the NASA Glenn robots route were checked before substantive access. The robots route allowed all public paths, no agent-specific restriction was found, and no access control was bypassed.

sources.title: Turbine Design and Application, Volume 1
sources.locator: Chapter 1, Equation of State: equations 1-1 through 1-4 and the accompanying discussion of ideal and real gas behavior
sources.license: Publicly distributed U.S. government technical publication; used only as a factual reference

uses.rightsBasisEvidence: The source was used to verify the qualitative low-pressure/high-temperature domain and the roles of molecular free space and attractive forces. No report wording, numerical example, chart, equation numbering, image, or table is reused.

agentAccess.notes: The NASA disclaimer and NTRS robots route were checked before substantive access. NTRS allowed all public paths; the report was publicly distributed and retrieved without login, paywall, rate-limit bypass, or other technical circumvention.
